explanation: Missing values occur when no data is stored for a particular feature in an observation. This can happen due to data entry errors, equipment failure, or survey non-response.
risk: Reduces the statistical power of the model and can lead to biased estimates if the missingness is not random. Most ML algorithms cannot handle NaN values directly.
detection_method: Use `pandas.isnull().sum()` to identify the count of missing entries per column.
suggested_fix: Imputation (Mean, Median, Mode), iterative imputation, or dropping rows/columns if the missingness is extensive.
example_scenario: A health survey where respondents skip the 'Income' question. If lower-income individuals skip the question more often, the remaining data will be skewed.

explanation: Bias in a dataset occurs when certain groups are unfairly represented or when labels are disproportionately assigned to one group over another.
risk: Models trained on biased data will perpetuate or amplify social inequalities, leading to unfair decisions in hiring, lending, or law enforcement.
detection_method: Calculate the difference in selection rates between groups (Demographic Parity Difference).
suggested_fix: Resampling (oversampling minority or undersampling majority), reweighing samples, or choosing fairness-aware algorithms.
example_scenario: An AI recruitment tool that has historically seen more male hires. If it uses this data, it will learn to favor male candidates over equally qualified female candidates.

explanation: Outliers are data points that differ significantly from other observations. They may be caused by measurement error or may indicate a genuine but rare phenomenon.
risk: Outliers significantly skew the mean and standard deviation, causing models like Linear Regression to perform poorly as they try to accommodate these extreme points.
detection_method: Z-score (std dev > 3) or Interquartile Range (IQR) method.
suggested_fix: Winsorizing (capping values), transformation (log scaling), or removal if they are confirmed as errors.
example_scenario: In a dataset of house prices, one entry is listed as $100 instead of $1,000,000 due to a typo. This will drastically pull down the average price predicted by the model.

explanation: Occurs when one class in the target variable significantly outnumbers the others.
risk: The model will achieve high accuracy by simply predicting the majority class every time, failing to identify the important minority class (e.g., fraud detection).
detection_method: Count class frequencies in the target column.
suggested_fix: SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique), adjusting class weights, or using precision-recall instead of accuracy as a metric.
example_scenario: In credit card fraud detection, 99.9% of transactions are legitimate. A model that always predicts 'not fraud' will be 99.9% accurate but useless.
